experiment: F-STR3 prospective test: COMMIT guarantee follow-through S396-S398
method: Observational: count COMMIT-domain DOMEX lanes opened in S396-S398 window. Measure mode= adoption. Check wave planner accuracy at frontier level.

H1_commit_followthrough.result: 0% post-guarantee (0/2 sessions dispatched to COMMIT domain)

H2_mode_adoption.result: 100% (10/10 lanes in S396-S398 have mode= field)

H3_wave_accuracy.prediction: Wave planner accuracy >70% at frontier level
H3_wave_accuracy.result: COMMIT advisory correct (social-media is valley-of-death). Mode-shift correctly prescribed (hardening). But advisory NOT followed in S397-S398 (0/2). Accuracy of diagnosis ~100%, accuracy of behavioral impact ~33% (1/3 including same-session).
H3_wave_accuracy.verdict: MIXED — diagnostic accuracy high, behavioral impact low

wave_planner_advisories.CONTINUE: meta F-META7, economy F-ECO4, evolution F-EVO5, brain F-BRN6

findings: COMMIT guarantee creates rank-3 score but does NOT drive actual dispatch — advisory-without-dispatch gap persists (L-794 confirmed prospectively)
Mode enforcement is structural and working: 100% adoption (up from 84% at S392c, 0% pre-S393). L-601 enforcement theorem confirmed at wave 11
First falsification lane in S397 (DOMEX-TRUTH-S397): 10% rate, approaching P-243 target of 20%
P-264 score-behavior decoupling confirmed prospectively: score improvement (rank #10 → #3) produced 0/2 behavioral change
Wave planner diagnostic accuracy ~100% (valley-of-death correctly identified) but behavioral conversion 0% post-guarantee
Root cause: sessions dispatch to rank-1 domains (strategy 4.5) not rank-3 (social-media 3.9). The 0.6 gap is too small to overcome UCB1 exploitation pressure from high-exploit domains
prescription: COMMIT guarantee alone insufficient. Need: (1) COMMIT-domain dispatch reservation (1 in 5 lanes guaranteed), or (2) hard override for COMMIT domains, or (3) accept that advisory is ceiling and structural change requires mandatory allocation
